evals.prompt: Integrate Claude API into our NestJS backend for a content generation feature. Need: streaming responses, function calling for structured output, token usage tracking, and fallback to a cached response if the API is down.
evals.expected_output: NestJS service with Claude API integration, streaming, function calling, and fallback

assertions.name: claude_api_client
assertions.description: Anthropic SDK properly initialized with API key from env

assertions.name: streaming
assertions.description: Streaming response support for real-time content delivery

assertions.name: function_calling
assertions.description: Tool use / function calling for structured output

assertions.name: token_tracking
assertions.description: Input/output token usage logged or tracked

assertions.name: fallback
assertions.description: Graceful degradation when API is unavailable

evals.prompt: Build a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline. Documents are stored in PostgreSQL with pgvector embeddings. User asks a question, we search for relevant chunks, then pass them to Claude as context for the answer.
evals.expected_output: RAG pipeline with embedding search, context assembly, and Claude API call

assertions.name: embedding_search
assertions.description: Vector similarity search using pgvector

assertions.name: context_assembly
assertions.description: Retrieved chunks assembled into a prompt context

assertions.name: claude_call
assertions.description: Claude API called with context + user question

assertions.name: source_attribution
assertions.description: Response includes references to source documents

assertions.name: chunk_limit
assertions.description: Context limited to avoid exceeding token limits

evals.prompt: Add AI-powered content moderation to our user-generated content platform. When a user posts a comment, check it with Claude for: spam, harassment, NSFW content, and PII exposure. Flag or auto-reject based on severity.
evals.expected_output: Moderation pipeline with structured classification, severity levels, and auto-reject logic

assertions.name: classification
assertions.description: Classifies content into categories (spam, harassment, NSFW, PII)

assertions.name: severity_levels
assertions.description: Each category has severity levels determining action

assertions.name: auto_reject
assertions.description: High-severity content auto-rejected

assertions.name: flag_for_review
assertions.description: Medium-severity flagged for human review

assertions.name: async_processing
assertions.description: Moderation runs async, not blocking the post creation

evals.prompt: Implement prompt caching for our AI feature. We make similar API calls with the same system prompt + different user queries. Cache the system prompt portion to reduce costs and latency.
evals.expected_output: Prompt caching implementation that reuses system prompt tokens across requests

assertions.name: cache_control
assertions.description: Uses Anthropic prompt caching with cache_control markers

assertions.name: system_prompt_cached
assertions.description: System prompt marked for caching

assertions.name: cost_reduction
assertions.description: Explains expected cost savings from caching

assertions.name: cache_ttl
assertions.description: Understands cache TTL and when cache misses occur

assertions.name: monitoring
assertions.description: Tracks cache hit/miss rate

evals.prompt: Build a multi-model routing system. Simple queries go to Haiku (fast, cheap), complex queries go to Sonnet, and critical queries go to Opus. Route based on query complexity analysis.
evals.expected_output: Model router that classifies query complexity and routes to appropriate model

assertions.name: three_models
assertions.description: Routes to Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus based on complexity

assertions.name: complexity_classification
assertions.description: Classifies queries as simple/complex/critical

assertions.name: cost_optimization
assertions.description: Simple queries use cheapest model to save costs

assertions.name: fallback_chain
assertions.description: Falls back to more capable model if cheaper model fails

assertions.name: latency_tracking
assertions.description: Tracks response time per model for monitoring
